result.lever: LEVER B — RQ source-eligibility enforcement (source-type / language / recency) at the citable generation-pool boundary + upstream primary-source citation re-anchoring
result.byte_identity_off: Every new flag defaults to '' (off) in config_defaults.py, read via settings.resolve(). (1) build_rq_eligibility() returns an EMPTY RQEligibilityPlan whenever PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_ENFORCE is off OR protocol['_rq_constraints'] is absent OR the RQ states no source/language/recency constraint -> url_to_eligibility_weight={} and _ineligible_urls=set(). In evidence_selector, an empty map makes _eligibility_weight(row) return 1.0 for every row, so the multiplicative sort-key factor is 1.0 (identity) on BOTH the redesign and legacy floor-path sort keys and on the capped-path score demotion; the tail-partition union (_oow_urls|_oos_urls|_ineligible_urls) is unchanged because _ineligible_urls is empty; and the disclosure note is only appended when url_to_eligibility_weight is truthy. The build call is wrapped in try/except that fails open to empty on any error, exactly mirroring the proven scope seam. (2) reanchor_enabled() off -> reanchor_citation returns current_ev_id unchanged and reanchor_sentences returns list(sentences) unchanged. A unit check (scratchpad/check_leverb.py) proves selection order with the flag off equals the no-_rq_constraints run identically, and that the off re-anchor returns identical tokens.
result.generalization: No task-72 literals. The eligibility policy reads the RQ's OWN parsed constraints from protocol['_rq_constraints'] (the previously-orphaned instruction/constraint_extractor.py output: source_types/languages/recency), using the SAME canonical source-type vocabulary constraint_extractor already emits for ANY prompt. Source-type eligibility maps those canonical tokens to DocumentType genre families via the field-agnostic document_type_classifier (no clinical/AI-labor branch); language uses a fail-open row-side ISO-639-1 signal; recency extracts the min 4-digit year from whatever recency phrase the RQ stated. Any row with an unresolved genre/language/date is fail-open (weight 1.0) and flagged for fetch recovery, never punished. Re-anchoring decides primary-vs-secondary generically from tier ordinal + document genre + DOI presence plus a numeric/keyword same-fact match of the claim's own text, so it only ever strengthens grounding for the identical numbers. An RQ that states no such constraint yields an empty plan for every domain.
result.summary: Wired the orphaned RQ constraint_extractor into a task-derived source-eligibility policy that DEMOTES (weight-not-drop, tail-partitioned, kept + disclosed, unresolved rows flagged for fetch recovery) ineligible citable rows at the generation-pool boundary, mirroring the proven date/scope WEIGHT seam. Changes: config_defaults.py adds PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_ENFORCE (~L723), PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_DEMOTE_WEIGHT (~L725), PG_CITATION_REANCHOR_PRIMARY (~L116). New rq_eligibility.py builds the per-URL eligibility demote plan from protocol['_rq_constraints']. evidence_selector._select_evidence_for_generation_impl: build map (~L3068), capped-path score demotion (~L3196), thread url_to_eligibility_weight into _relevance_floor_selection call (~L3276) + its signature (~L2237) + new _eligibility_weight helper (~L2362) folded into both floor sort keys, disclosure note (~L3324), and tail-partition union now includes _ineligible_urls (~L3344). New planning/citation_reanchor.py re-points a claim's [#ev] token from a secondary to a strictly-more-primary same-fact row BEFORE strict_verify (upstream grounding-source selection, no sentence filtering). No post-generation entailment/verify/drop gate added. All four files py_compile clean; unit check proves off=byte-identical no-op and on-path actually fires (treatment, not env presence).

result.lever: LEVER E (fetch-until-usable): a sub-floor stub fetch row carrying a DOI or PMID is routed through a SHARED deterministic metadata-API salvage lane (CrossRef -> OpenAlex -> PubMed -> Semantic Scholar richest abstract) to recover a citable span BEFORE it is admitted as a stub. Upstream fetch-layer only; recovery ADDS content, never drops.
new_flags: PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE (default '' = off; master gate for the whole lane)

PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MIN_BODY_CHARS (default '800'; the real floor that defines a stub, consulted ONLY when the lane is on — leaves PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS at '0'/off so existing gates are untouched)
result.byte_identity_off: All three flags default to their current/off value and every new code path is guarded by _stub_salvage_enabled() (returns False when PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE is unset/'' or a recognized falsey value). The two wire-in sites (naive-path helper _apply_min_body_stub_gate ~line 3019 and primary branch in _fetch_content ~line 4475) each begin with `if _stub_salvage_enabled():` so when off the block is skipped entirely and control flow reaches the pre-existing F14 logic unchanged. I deliberately did NOT change PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS (kept '0'), so the existing F14 min-body gate and OA-resolver behavior stay byte-identical; LEVER E uses its OWN independent floor. frame_fetcher.salvage_stub_span is a brand-new function called nowhere unless the flag fires. git diff on the three files is +300 / -0 (pure additions). Verified: with the flag unset, lr._stub_salvage_enabled() is False, lr._try_stub_salvage returns ('', 'none'), and _apply_min_body_stub_gate returns its input tuple unchanged.
result.generalization: salvage_stub_span takes only (doi, pmid) and consults DOI/PMID-keyed public metadata APIs — no task-72 literals, no clinical/AI-labor/journal-pre-2023 branch, no domain packs. The trigger is purely structural: any fetched body below the char floor that carries a DOI (from the hint or _extract_doi_from_url) or a PMID. It reuses frame_fetcher's existing generic _call_crossref/_call_openalex/_call_pubmed/_call_s2 clients and the domain-agnostic _pick_richest_abstract selector. It is strictly a generalization of the same chain frame_fetcher already runs for V30 contract entities, lifted out of the EvidenceBinding/FrameRow coupling so it works for ANY stub row in any RQ/domain. It also broadens the existing live_retriever OA resolver (which required a DOI and only did Unpaywall+PubMed) to also salvage PMID-only rows and to consult CrossRef/OpenAlex/S2.
result.summary: Implemented LEVER E on branch fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff. (1) config_defaults.py: added three central defaults — PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE (''=off), PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MIN_BODY_CHARS ('800'), PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MAX_SOURCES ('4'); left PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS at '0' so existing gates stay byte-identical. (2) frame_fetcher.py: added salvage_stub_span(doi, pmid, client, max_sources) at ~line 1278 — a standalone, deterministic, fail-safe salvage that reuses the existing _call_crossref/_call_openalex/_call_pubmed/_call_s2 clients + _pick_richest_abstract to return (span, source_label); no new HTTP surface, no LLM call, and it handles the PMID-only case the OA resolver refuses. (3) live_retriever.py: added _stub_salvage_enabled()/_stub_salvage_min_body_chars()/_stub_salvage_max_sources()/_try_stub_salvage() (~line 205, all read via resolve()) and wired the lane into BOTH the naive-path helper _apply_min_body_stub_gate (~line 3019) and the primary _fetch_content branch (~line 4475): when the flag is on and the body is below the salvage floor, the recovered citable span replaces the shell with ok=True (status='ok', _m45 backend 'stub_salvage'); on a miss it keeps the existing stub verdict (retained for disclosure, not citation, §-1.3). No post-generation entailment/verification/filtering gate was added — upstream fetch-layer only. py_compile passed on all three files (unset PYTHONPATH, pipeline-env python). A 7-case pytest fixture passed: salvage picks the richest abstract, the PMID-only path works, no-ids returns ('','none'), flag-default-off is a no-op, flag-on fires and reads the 800/4 defaults, the gate helper is byte-identical when off, and the gate upgrades the tuple when on. The flag was verified to actually FIRE (not just be present). Nothing committed; no branch switch.

result.lever: LEVER F (canonicalize works) — the GLOBAL bibliography unit becomes the canonical WORK instead of the raw evidence_id, so a paper fetched from several mirror URLs / manifestations folds from [5][6][7] to a single [N], with every member evidence_id remapped and adjacent duplicate markers collapsed.
result.byte_identity_off: The flag default is '' (registered in CONFIG_DEFAULTS, read via resolve('PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY')). _canonical_work_bibliography_enabled() returns False for '' (not in the truthy set {1,true,yes,on}). When False: (a) _merge_bibliographies returns the legacy per-evidence_id list unchanged (the canonicalize call is gated behind the flag and never runs, so no 'same_work_member_evidence_ids' key is added — the biblio dict shape is byte-identical, asserted by test_off_is_byte_identical); (b) _remap_section_markers_to_global builds the plain {evidence_id: num} map exactly as before and never invokes _collapse_adjacent_duplicate_markers. No code path other than the two flag-gated branches is altered, so inline [N] markers and the bibliography are byte-identical when the flag is unset.
result.generalization: Work identity is computed by reusing finding_dedup._same_work_key — the SINGLE shared deterministic identity contract the upstream same-work consolidators already key on: normalized source URL first, else normalized DOI, else folded title + a corroborating discriminator (year/first-author/venue/host). It reads only the bibliography entry's OWN locators (url/doi/title/year/authors/venue) generically — there is NO task-72 literal, no journal-only/clinical-only/pre-2023/AI-labor branch, no domain switch. A blank key (no usable locator) falls back to 'ev:<evidence_id>' so an un-keyable entry becomes its own singleton and is NEVER merged into an unrelated work. The DOI>PMID>URL canonical-manifestation preference is pure metadata-presence tie-breaking. This works for any RQ/domain because it operates purely on locator identity carried by every evidence row.
result.summary: Implemented LEVER F behind PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY (default off, added to config_defaults.py and read via resolve()). In multi_section_generator.py: _merge_bibliographies now optionally folds same-work mirror entries into one canonical entry per work via _canonicalize_work_bibliography (grouping by the shared finding_dedup._same_work_key, electing the DOI/primary manifestation, renumbering contiguously in first-appearance order, and carrying same_work_member_evidence_ids); _remap_section_markers_to_global maps every member evidence_id onto its canonical [N] and collapses resulting adjacent duplicate markers via _collapse_adjacent_duplicate_markers. Pure deterministic identity+numbering — no model, no network, no sentence drop/filter (a folded mirror still resolves, it just shares a number). No FAITH-GHOST surface added; upstream/render-only. Every touched .py passes py_compile (unset PYTHONPATH, pipeline-env python). A 3-case pytest unit check proves off=byte-identical (including unchanged dict shape) and on=two DOI mirrors fold to one [N] with markers remapped and [1][1] collapsed to [1] — all pass; existing test_multi_section_gap4.py (9 tests) still passes. No commit, no branch switch; work confined to /home/polaris/wt/faithoff on fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate. The other modified files in git status (evidence_selector/frame_fetcher/live_retriever, citation_reanchor.py, rq_eligibility.py) are pre-existing Lever B/E work I did not touch.

Findings on the assigned dimension:

1. Config registration — all 7 new flags are registered in `config_defaults.py` with exactly one entry each: `PG_CITATION_REANCHOR_PRIMARY` (L121), `PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY` (L138), `PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE` (L389), `PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MIN_BODY_CHARS` (L396), `PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MAX_SOURCES` (L401), `PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_ENFORCE` (L781), `PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_DEMOTE_WEIGHT` (L785).

2. Read via `resolve()` — every new flag is read exclusively through `resolve()`, never a bare `os.getenv`/`os.environ` or literal. No added line in the tracked diff and no new untracked file contains a bare env read. (The `os.getenv`/`os.environ` grep hits are all in pre-existing, unchanged code.) All defaults are non-None strings (`''`, `'800'`, `'4'`), so the direct `.strip()` calls in `_stub_salvage_enabled()` and `_canonical_work_bibliography_enabled()` cannot hit a `None`; the eligibility/re-anchor accessors additionally guard with `(resolve(...) or "")`.

3. Plain names — all names are plain UPPER_SNAKE `PG_*`, no RQ/task-specific tokens baked into any name.

4. Documented — each flag has a preceding explanatory comment block in `config_defaults.py`.

5. No task-72/clinical-only literals in new code — the RQ is read generically. The only `task-72` occurrence in the diff is descriptive prose inside a config comment (config_defaults.py L17 of the hunk: "55 same_work_groups on task-72"); the pre-existing `clinical`/`GLP-1` grep hits are all in unchanged lines, not Batch-1 additions. rq_eligibility drives entirely off `protocol['_rq_constraints']` (constraint_extractor output) and the field-agnostic `document_type_classifier`; citation_reanchor decides primary/secondary from generic tier/genre/DOI signals.

6. Flags are live, not dead — `PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_DEMOTE_WEIGHT` is consumed via `demote_weight()` (rq_eligibility.py L256), and evidence_selector reaches eligibility only through `build_rq_eligibility` (the two `PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_*` mentions in evidence_selector are comments only).

No checkpoint serialization touches these structures — `_merge_bibliographies`/`_remap` run at final assembly (line 12033), not across a resume boundary. Checkpoint-safe.

I have completed the review across all four levers on the FAITH-GHOST + DATA-SAFETY dimension. Summary of findings.

---

CLEAN on the primary FAITH-GHOST + DATA-SAFETY dimension, with two DATA-SAFETY caveats worth flagging (neither is a post-generation verify/drop gate, and neither deletes an evidence row from the corpus — but one narrows what the reader sees).

**FAITH-GHOST — no post-generation entailment/verify/drop gate added anywhere.** Verified all four levers:
- LEVER E (`live_retriever.py:3018-3040`, `4473-4519`, `frame_fetcher.py:1278-1405`): upstream fetch-layer recovery only. It ADDS content (upgrades a sub-floor shell to a citable span) and returns the tuple unchanged on a miss (`live_retriever.py:3040`, `4519`). Fail-safe swallows every error → `("", "none")`. No verify/entailment/sentence-drop. Correct.
- LEVER B eligibility (`evidence_selector.py:2352-2373, 2525-2542, 3057-3086, 3189-3206, 3341-3345`, `rq_eligibility.py`): pure WEIGHT-not-drop. Ineligible rows get a multiplicative demote (default 0.3, clamped (0.01,0.99) at `rq_eligibility.py:94`), are tail-partitioned and KEPT (`evidence_selector.py:3341`). Floor comparison uses raw relevance, not the demote weight. Unresolved rows fail-open at weight 1.0. No drop, no gate.
- LEVER F (`multi_section_generator.py:8218-8285`): deterministic identity/renumber only. No model, no network, no sentence drop.
- Re-anchor (`citation_reanchor.py`): swaps ev-ids only when a strictly-more-primary row grounds the SAME numbers; runs BEFORE strict_verify; never drops a sentence/citation.

**Checkpoint-safe.** `_merge_bibliographies` / `_remap_section_markers_to_global` run at final assembly (`multi_section_generator.py:12033-12034`), not across a resume boundary; no checkpoint/serializer touches `biblio_slice`, `global_biblio`, or the new `same_work_member_evidence_ids` key. LEVER E / B run in the live retrieval + selection pass. No resume-path hazard.

DATA-SAFETY caveats (report-only, not blocking on this dimension):

1. `src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:8285` (LEVER F) — mild "weight-not-drop" tension. When ON, N per-mirror bibliography *rows* are collapsed into ONE canonical entry; the folded mirrors' distinct URLs/locators are removed from the *rendered* bibliography. Failure scenario: a work fetched from 3 mirror URLs renders as one `[5]` pointing at only the DOI-bearing manifestation's URL — the other two source URLs no longer appear anywhere in output. Evidence_ids are preserved in `same_work_member_evidence_ids` and still resolve internally, so this is not corpus deletion, but the reader loses the alternate-locator disclosure. Default OFF, so no live impact today.

2. `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/rq_eligibility.py:231` — LEVER B eligibility is currently INERT (safe, but a wiring gap, not a safety violation): `protocol['_rq_constraints']` is never populated anywhere in `src/` (grep confirms only comments reference it). With `PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_ENFORCE=1` set, `build_rq_eligibility` returns an empty plan → byte-identical selection. This means the demote lever silently does nothing until an upstream writer (not in this diff) caches the constraints. On the DATA-SAFETY dimension this is fail-safe (empty plan = no demote), but the lever cannot demote anything as shipped.

Non-dimension note (efficacy, not requested but load-bearing for LEVER F): the LEVER F rationale ("a paper cited as [5][6][7]" from mirror URLs folds) relies on the DOI leg of `_same_work_key`, but `PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG` is DEFAULT ON and `_normalize_source_url` (finding_dedup.py:442) reads `url`, so distinct mirror URLs yield distinct `url:` keys and do NOT fold in production. The test masks this by forcing `PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG=0` (test file line 44). Not a faith/safety issue — flagging because the shipped-default behavior diverges from the design comment.

No evidence rows are deleted by any lever (E adds; B demotes+keeps+partitions; F folds-but-preserves-ev_ids; re-anchor swaps-in-place). No post-generation verify/entailment/drop gate is introduced. CLEAN on FAITH-GHOST; the two DATA-SAFETY items above are disclosure/wiring caveats, both gated OFF by default.
